q: Does the sign that ends all local prohibitions also put an end to parking signs?

LESSON 26
The sign with a black diagonal stripe informs road users of the end of all regulations previously indicated by signs, except those concerning stopping and parking.

q:
From 11:30 a.m. to 7:30 p.m., stopping and parking are prohibited.

A. Only on the carriageway.
B. On the carriageway and on the verge.
e:
LESSON 26
The sign prohibits stopping and parking on both the carriageway and the verge.
If there are numbers on the sign, the prohibition applies only to the carriageway.

q:
Today is 12 February.
What is the car allowed to do at this place?

A. Stop and park.
B. Stop only.
C. Neither stop nor park.
e:
LESSON 26
This traffic sign means that parking is prohibited on this side of the carriageway from the 1st to the 15th of the month.
The additional plate with a downward arrow indicates that the parking prohibition applies up to the sign.

q:
The driver of the car has stopped to let the child get in.
Is that allowed at this place?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 26
There is a broken white line between the traffic lanes. That means parking is not allowed there.
But stopping to let someone get in is allowed.

q:
A priority road outside a built-up area.
May the blue car stop on the roadway to let a passenger get in?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 26
Inside a built-up area, you may stop on the roadway and also park there if it is not divided into lanes.
Outside a built-up area, you may stop on the roadway, but not park.

q:
This sign is above the sign: start of a built-up area. It's March 7th. Where can you park?

A. Along the side of the odd house numbers.
B. Along the side of the even house numbers.
e:
The sign semi-monthly parking is actually no longer applied. That is why it is no longer on our theory pages. If you still have questions about it, here is the explanation:
The half-monthly alternating parking is in effect on all carriageways in a built-up area, when this traffic sign is placed above the traffic signs indicating the start of this built-up area. Parking on the road is then only allowed from the 1st to the 15th of the month, on the side of the buildings with odd numbers and, from the 16th to the end of the month, on the side of the buildings with even numbers.
clean_q:

B. Along the side of the even house numbers.
clean_e:
The sign semi-monthly parking is actually no longer applied. That is why it is no longer on our theory pages. If you still have questions about it, here is the explanation:

Parking on the road is then only allowed from the 1st to the 15th of the month, on the side of the buildings with odd numbers and, from the 16th to the end of the month, on the side of the buildings with even numbers.

q:
The red car is parked at this place. Is this allowed?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 26
This traffic sign means that parking is prohibited on this side of the carriageway and also on the verge.
The additional plate with the black downward arrow indicates that the parking prohibition ends at the sign.
➡️ However: beyond the sign, the carriageway is divided into two lanes by a broken white line.
On such a carriageway, parking is not allowed.
➡️ Stopping (for example to allow a passenger to get in or out) is allowed.
clean_q:
The red car is parked at this place. Is this allowed?
A. Yes.
B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 26
This traffic sign means that parking is prohibited on this side of the carriageway and also on the verge.
The additional plate with the black downward arrow indicates that the parking prohibition ends at the sign.
➡️ However: beyond the sign, the carriageway is divided into two lanes by a broken white line.
On such a carriageway, parking is not allowed.
➡️ Stopping (for example to allow a passenger to get in or out) is allowed.

q:
A yellow broken line at the edge of the carriageway.
This car may stop to let someone get out.
But is the driver also allowed to park there?
e:
LESSON 26
When a yellow broken line is painted at the edge of the carriageway, you may only stop.
Parking on the carriageway is prohibited.
